title: Pass 5.B.1 follow-up: dirichlet sample/log_prob/entropy fail-fast on CUDA via fallback.rs (Pattern A)
description:
Pass 5.B.1 (#1103) migrated transforms.rs to device-resident ops (Pattern B). dirichlet.rs cannot follow Pattern B in this dispatch because its scalar bodies depend on `lgamma_t` and `digamma_t` (special_fns.rs), which have no GPU equivalent in ferrotorch-core.

The fallback.rs gate is already wired into every dirichlet method, so on Linux/CPU runs nothing changes. The remaining work is to keep the fallback.rs guards in place and document that full Pattern B migration is blocked on GPU lgamma/digamma kernels. PyTorch parity: torch.distributions.Dirichlet on CUDA also routes through CPU; we just want the error to be loud (already is).

Sites already guarded by check_gpu_fallback_opt_in (Pattern A is in place):
- dirichlet.rs:124 Dirichlet::sample (alpha readback at line 128, recompose at 147)
- dirichlet.rs:156 Dirichlet::rsample (alpha at 160, recompose at 180-184)
- dirichlet.rs:203 Dirichlet::log_prob (alpha+value at 210-211, recompose at 254)
- dirichlet.rs:265 Dirichlet::mean (alpha at 267, recompose at 272)
- dirichlet.rs:286 Dirichlet::variance (alpha at 288, recompose at 299)
- dirichlet.rs:311 Dirichlet::entropy (alpha at 317, recompose at 336)

Acceptance:
- New finding documents that Pattern A is the production posture for dirichlet until GPU lgamma/digamma kernels land.
- When ferrotorch-core grows lgamma/digamma kernels (separate work), revisit this issue and migrate to Pattern B.
- No code change in this issue beyond verifying the existing fallback.rs guards remain.

Related:
- #982 audit umbrella
- #1103 transforms.rs Pattern B migration (this issue's parent dispatch)
